|  First game         = {{fb|SRI}} 3–2 {{fb-rt|TLS}}<br>([[Colombo]], [[Sri Lanka]]; 21 March 2003)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.eloratings.net/East_Timor.htm|title=East Timor matches, ratings and points exchanged|publisher=World Football Elo Ratings: East Timor|access-date=24 November 2016|archive-date=23 November 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161123222613/http://www.eloratings.net/East_Timor.htm|url-status=live}}</ref>
|  Largest win        = {{fb|CAM}} 1–5 {{fb-rt|TLS}}<br>([[Yangon]], [[Myanmar]]; 5 October 2012)
|  Largest loss       = {{fb|TLS}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round#Group A|0–10]] {{fb-rt|KSA}}<br>([[Dili]], [[Timor Leste]]; 17 November 2015)<br />{{fb|TLS}} [[2024 ASEAN Championship Group A#East Timor vs Thailand|0–10]] {{fb-rt|THA}}<br />([[Hanoi]], [[Vietnam]]; 8 December 2024)
| Regional name = [[AFC Solidarity Cup]]
| Regional cup apps = 1
| Regional cup first = [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup|2016]]
| Regional cup best = Group stage ([[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup|2016]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[ASEAN Championship]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 4
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2004 AFF Championship|2004]]
| 2ndRegional cup best    = Group stage ([[2004 AFF Championship|2004]], [[2018 AFF Championship|2018]], [[2020 AFF Championship|2020]], [[2024 ASEAN Championship|2024]])
}}

The '''Timor-Leste national football team''' ({{Langx|pt|Seleção Timorense de Futebol}}) represents [[Timor-Leste]] in the senior men's international football. It is organised by the [[East Timor Football Federation|Federação de Futebol de Timor-Leste]] (FFTL) which is affiliated with the [[Asian Football Confederation]] (AFC) and the regional [[ASEAN Football Federation]] (AFF). They became a member of [[FIFA]] on 12 September 2005.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/association=tls/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070606225505/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=tls/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 June 2007|title=Member Association – Timor-Leste|website=FIFA.com|language=en-GB|access-date=2018-09-04}}</ref> The team's traditional home kit is primarily faded red, often paired with black shorts and sometimes accented with yellow trim. Timor-Leste are colloquially known as "Lafaek" also known as 'The Crocodiles', an animal of prominent symbolic nature to the country.

Timor-Leste's international debut was in the preliminary round of the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] in March 2003, when they lost 3–2 to [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] and 3–0 to [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://us.soccerway.com/teams/east-timor/timor-leste/3844/|title=Timor-Leste – Timor-Leste – Results, fixtures, squad, statistics, photos, videos and news – Soccerway|website=us.soccerway.com|language=en|access-date=2018-09-04|archive-date=13 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190913170909/https://us.soccerway.com/teams/east-timor/timor-leste/3844/|url-status=live}}</ref> Timor-Leste won their first international match on 5 October 2012 against [[Cambodian national football team|Cambodia]] with a score of 5–1.<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 October 2012 |title=Timor Leste cause upset; Myanmar win |url=http://www.espnstar.com/football/aff-suzuki-cup/news/detail/item868331/Timor-Leste-stun-Cambodia-in-qualifiers/ |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121012083626/http://www.espnstar.com/football/aff-suzuki-cup/news/detail/item868331/Timor-Leste-stun-Cambodia-in-qualifiers/ |archive-date=12 October 2012 |website=ESPNStar.com |df=dmy-all}}</ref>

==History==

=== Early history (2003–2017) ===
Football was established in East Timor during the Portuguese colonial era, when many of the locals and the Portuguese officials played amateur football. After Portugal ended its colonial rule, [[Indonesia]] invaded and occupied the island, which resulted in a long running battle against Jakarta-led forces. Timor-Leste eventually gained independence from Indonesia in 2002.

The [[East Timor Football Federation]] was accepted as an associate member of AFC at the 20th AFC Congress in 2002. Their international debut came in March 2003 when they participated in the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] campaign for the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup]] in [[China]]. Given their history, it was no surprise that they crashed out with defeats against the likes of [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] and [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]]. They then entered the regional competition for the first time in 2004 as they played in the [[2004 AFF Championship]] as an invited member. They finished in last place, showing that they had a long way to go before they could compete even on the regional stage.

They again had no success in the [[2007 AFF Championship qualification]], with four heavy losses, including a 7–0 trouncing by the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]]. The following year in the [[2008 AFF Championship qualification]], Timor-Leste surprisingly drew against [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]]; this feat was reported in international news.

==== First win ====
In the [[2012 AFF Championship qualification]], the country's [[Federação de Futebol de Timor-Leste|football federation (FFTL)]] reportedly selected their foreign based players, who played in Brazil, Portugal, Australia and elsewhere, to fortify the team as they also did at the [[Football at the 2011 Southeast Asian Games|2011 SEA Games]].<ref>{{Cite news|date=23 May 2012|title=KIMURA FOR LAOS' AFF SUZUKI CUP 2012 QUALIFYING CAMPAIGN|work=ASEAN Football|url=https://www.aseanfootball.org/v3/kimura-for-laos-aff-suzuki-cup-2012-qualifying-campaign/|access-date=22 May 2020|archive-date=30 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200730212824/https://www.aseanfootball.org/v3/kimura-for-laos-aff-suzuki-cup-2012-qualifying-campaign/|url-status=live}}</ref>
[[File:Coach Emerson Alcantara.jpg|left|thumb|227x227px|[[Emerson Alcântara]] lead East Timor to a 5–1 victory over [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] in their first-ever win in 2012.]]
On 5 October 2012, Timor-Leste won their first international match in a 5–1 victory against [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] with goals coming from [[Murilo de Almeida]] who scored a brace, [[Adelino Trindade]] and Alan Leandro.<ref>{{cite web|title=Cambodia 1-5 Timor Leste|url=http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/cambodia-1-timor-leste-5|date=5 October 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130411035051/http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/cambodia-1-timor-leste-5|archive-date=11 April 2013|access-date=21 May 2020|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 9 October 2012, the team won another match against [[Laos national football team|Laos]] in the qualification round as [[Murilo de Almeida]] scored a penalty at 43' minutes of the first half of the match. Later [[Adelino Trindade]] extended the Timorese advantage with a header early in the second half and then Alan Leandro added the third goal on the 83rd minute.<ref>{{cite web|title=Timor Leste 3 Laos 1|url=http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/timor-leste-3-laos-1|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121011061336/http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/timor-leste-3-laos-1|archive-date=11 October 2012|access-date=2012-10-11|df=dmy-all}}</ref> After winning the second match against Laos, head coach [[Emerson Alcântara]] stated "This is a win for the people of East Timor. They had to wait a long time for our team to win their first match in this competition but now we have two wins and this was an important victory for Timor-Leste. "I think that about 70 percent of people in Timor Leste are poor so it is very important to get this result for them because they love football and the people can get confidence and take pride in this result. It is very important for us to motivate our people and to help to change their lives."<ref>{{Cite news|date=10 October 2012|title=Win dedicated to Timorese people|url=http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/win-dedicated-to-timorese-people|url-status=live|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180416135557/http://www.affsuzukicup.com/news/win-dedicated-to-timorese-people|archive-date=16 April 2018}}</ref> With a win needed in the final fixture, Timor-Leste subsequently lost to [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] 2–1 and finished in third place in the group, one point away from runners-up of the group, Laos as they went on to qualify for the [[2012 AFF Championship]]. In the [[2014 AFF Championship qualification]], Timor-Leste secured a victory against Brunei winning them 4–2 in the process. In the next match, they caused an upset to [[Myanmar national football team|Myanmar]] on 14 October 2014 which ended up in a goalless draw but that wasn't enough as Timor-Leste didn't make it to the [[2014 AFF Championship]].

==== 2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round ====
Timor-Leste then participated in the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification first round]] facing [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] where on 12 March 2015, they secured their first ever win against a country outside of [[Southeast Asia]] in a 4–1 win at the [[National Stadium (Timor-Leste)|National Stadium of Timor-Leste]]. In the second leg, Timor-Leste travelled to [[Ulaanbaatar]] where they won 1–0 putting them on an aggregate of 5–2 seeing them advancing to the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round]] however on 12 December 2017, FIFA awarded both matches 3–0 to Mongolia due to Timor-Leste fielding numerous ineligible players. However, this was long after the Second round had been played, so Timor-Leste advanced and Mongolia were not reinstated. Timor-Leste were then grouped with [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]], [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]], [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] and [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] in Group A. On 11 June 2015, [[Ramon Saro]] scored in the 90+3' minute stoppage time which cause a huge upsets to Malaysia in a 1–1 draw at the [[Bukit Jalil National Stadium]] which Timor-Leste got their first ever point in the campaign. On 8 October 2015 while playing at home, [[Ramon Saro]] break the deadlock as they lead against Palestine however in the dying minute of the match, [[Ahmad Abu Nahyeh]] equalise for Palestine in 1–1 draw as it gains media attention from the Southeast Asia region. However, Timor-Leste has to forfeited five of their group matches due to fielding numerous ineligible players. Since their last match against [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]] on 17 November 2015, Timor-Leste has been playing their home match overboard as their [[National Stadium (Timor-Leste)|National Stadium of Timor-Leste]] in [[Dili]] failed to meet FIFA standards which also see them finishing at the bottom of the table. During the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification – play-off round|2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] match, Timor-Leste played their home match at the [[Tan Sri Dato Haji Hassan Yunos Stadium]] in [[Johor Bahru]] playing against Malaysia. 
 
Timor-Leste then participated in the [[2016 AFC Solidarity Cup]] held in [[Kuching]] where they snatched a goalless draw against [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]]. They were also invited to the [[CTFA International Tournament|2017 CTFA International Tournament]] where in the match against the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]], [[Silveiro Garcia]] scored the only goal in the match as Timor-Leste gains a win in the tournament.

=== Back-to-back AFF Championships (2018–2020) ===
[[File:East Timor-Brunei 8 October 2024 22.jpg|thumb|288x288px|Timor Leste playing against regional rivals, [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]] during the [[2024 ASEAN Championship qualification]].]]
Timor-Leste then qualified to the [[2018 AFF Championship]] for the first time in 14 years winning the [[2018 AFF Championship qualification]] against Brunei on an aggregate of 3–2. They will play their designated "home" match against [[Thailand national football team|Thailand]] at the [[Rajamangala Stadium]] in Bangkok, while their home tie against the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] will be played at the [[Kuala Lumpur Stadium]] in Malaysia due to the incomplete floodlighting of the National Stadium of Timor-Leste. Timor-Leste then qualified for the [[2020 AFF Championship]] automatically after the withdrawal of Brunei in the qualification play-off.

=== Young star in the making (2021–present) ===
During the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – play-off round]] first leg match on 5 September 2024, [[João Pedro (footballer, born June 1998)|João Pedro]] became the first player in Timorese history to score a hat-trick in a 4–1 win over [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] while playing on neutral ground at the [[Kapten I Wayan Dipta Stadium]] held in [[Gianyar]], [[Bali]]. In October 2024, Timor Leste then played against Brunei who is also on a five-game winning streak for the [[2024 ASEAN Championship qualification]]. Timor Leste ended up victorious winning 1–0 on aggregate where [[Gali Freitas]] scored the goal to ensure the team qualified for the [[2024 ASEAN Championship]], where they will play their matches at [[Hàng Đẫy Stadium]] in [[Hanoi]].

==== 2024 ASEAN Championship ====
As of 28 December 2024, Timor-Leste's performance at the [[2024 ASEAN Championship]] has been poor, ending in a string of defeats that resulted in their early elimination. The squad competed in four matches, beginning with a devastating loss to [[Thailand]] on 8 December suffering their worst ever defeat since 2015 with a 10–0 thrasing defeat scoreline.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Thailand 10-0 Timor-Leste (Dec 8, 2024) Final Score |url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/702666/thailand-timor-leste |access-date=2024-12-28 |website=ESPN |language=en}}</ref> Following that, they met [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] on 11 December, putting up a valiant battle but ultimately lost 2–3. Their difficulties continued against [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]] on 14 December with a 0–3 defeat. Timor-Leste met [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] in their last group stage match on 17 December and fell to a 1–2 defeat.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Cambodia edges Timor-Leste in 2024 ASEAN Championship clash |url=https://english.news.cn/asiapacific/20241217/53c60d75c72d4c65ab4245add03c17e0/c.html |access-date=2024-12-28 |website=english.news.cn}}</ref> 

Timor-Leste finished with a record of four losses, two goals scored, and an incredible eighteen goals conceded, resulting in a goal differential of -16. They were unable to gain any victories during the tournament. They became the first team to be eliminated from the 2024 ASEAN Championship.<ref>{{Cite web |last=ONLINE |first=TUOI TRE |date=2024-12-14 |title=Thua đậm Singapore, tuyển Timor Leste chính thức bị loại tại ASEAN Cup 2024 |url=https://tuoitre.vn/thua-dam-singapore-tuyen-timor-leste-chinh-thuc-bi-loai-tai-asean-cup-2024-20241214194350901.htm |access-date=2024-12-28 |website=TUOI TRE ONLINE |language=vi}}</ref>

During the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|third round of 2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] on 10 June 2025, [[João Pedro (footballer, born 1998)|João Pedro]] scored the lone goal in a 1–0 win against [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]] which put him to become the all time top scorer for Timor-Leste.

==Eligibility of players controversy==
Since 2012, several [[Brazil]]ian-born footballers, who are not of East Timorese descent and had not necessarily played in the East Timorese League, were expressly naturalised.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://thesefootballtimes.co/2016/11/29/how-timor-leste-like-others-probably-flouted-fifas-eligibility-rules/|title=How Timor-Leste, like others, (probably) flouted FIFA's eligibility rules|date=2016-11-29|work=These Football Times|access-date=2018-09-04|language=en-GB|archive-date=4 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180904225641/https://thesefootballtimes.co/2016/11/29/how-timor-leste-like-others-probably-flouted-fifas-eligibility-rules/|url-status=live}}</ref>

The country's recent history of naturalising foreign players such as [[Murilo de Almeida]], [[Fellipe Bertoldo]], [[Diogo Rangel]], [[Rodrigo Silva (footballer, born 1987)|Rodrigo Silva]], [[Jairo Neto]], [[Ramon Saro]], Alan Leandro, [[Thiago Cunha]], [[Juninho (footballer, born May 1989)|Juninho]] and [[Heberty]] has been criticized by many, including native East Timorese players and fans.<ref>{{cite web |title=Timor Leste naturalisation of Brazilian footballers criticized |url=http://footballchannel.asia/2015/10/03/post7106/ |work=Football Channel Asia |date=3 October 2015 |access-date=4 October 2015 |archive-date=3 October 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151003195241/http://footballchannel.asia/2015/10/03/post7106/ |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/02/sports/soccer/how-did-east-timor-soccer-improve-so-much-brazilians.html?_r=1|title=Success Is No Longer Foreign to East Timor, but the Players Are|author=Jack Kerr|work=[[The New York Times]]|date=1 October 2015|access-date=3 March 2017|archive-date=17 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180817193416/https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/02/sports/soccer/how-did-east-timor-soccer-improve-so-much-brazilians.html?_r=1|url-status=live}}</ref>

In a match on 8 October 2015 against [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] seven of the starting eleven for the Timorese line-up were naturalized Brazilians.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/09/sports/soccer/east-timor-fields-seven-ex-brazilians-in-tie-with-palestine.html?_r=0|title=East Timor Fields Seven Ex-Brazilians in Tie With Palestine|author=Jack Kerr|work=The New York Times|date=8 October 2015|access-date=13 November 2015|archive-date=6 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170906092837/https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/09/sports/soccer/east-timor-fields-seven-ex-brazilians-in-tie-with-palestine.html?_r=0|url-status=live}}</ref> Following the match the [[Palestinian Football Association]] made a complaint to FIFA stating that the naturalized Timorese players were not eligible to represent Timor-Leste under FIFA rules.<ref>{{cite news |author=Jack Kerr |date=16 October 2015 |title=Palestinians Protest East Timor's Use of Brazilian Players |work=The New York Times |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/17/sports/soccer/palestine-protests-east-timors-use-of-brazilian-players.html |access-date=13 November 2015 |archive-date=12 December 2021 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20211212040533/https://www.nytimes.com/2015/10/17/sports/soccer/palestine-protests-east-timors-use-of-brazilian-players.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.vice.com/en/article/is-east-timor-illegally-putting-together-a-national-soccer-team-with-brazilian-players/|title=Is East Timor Illegally Putting Together a National Soccer Team With Brazilian Players?|date=2015-07-13|work=Sports|access-date=2018-09-04|language=en-au|archive-date=4 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180904192057/https://sports.vice.com/en_au/article/d7bmda/is-east-timor-illegally-putting-together-a-national-soccer-team-with-brazilian-players|url-status=live}}</ref>

Following a concern from some Timorese who complained to the prime minister, the prosecutor general and the minister for justice regarding the naturalisation program, [[Federação de Futebol de Timor-Leste|Timor Leste Football Federation]] (FFTL) decided to drop their overseas-born legion. It meant Timor-Leste would be without seven naturalised [[Brazil]]ian players for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2018 FIFA World Cup]] and [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification|2019 AFC Asian Cup]] joint qualifiers match against [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]] on 12 November 2015.<ref name="timor121115">{{cite web|title=Timor Leste set to be without seven naturalised Brazilians for UAE clash|url=http://footballchannel.asia/2015/11/12/post7867/|work=Football Channel Asia|date=12 November 2015|access-date=12 November 2015|archive-date=17 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151117033629/http://footballchannel.asia/2015/11/12/post7867/|url-status=dead}}</ref> In that match, coach [[Fernando Alcântara]] played an all national line up including six who were under twenty, including [[Ervino Soares]] who was 16. Timor-Leste ended up losing the match 8–0. Alcântara took responsibility for the defeat although he also added that he had been forced to play such an inexperienced line-up by the FFTL.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thenationalnews.com/sport/coach-mahdi-ali-cautions-his-players-that-tougher-challenges-await-as-uae-thrash-east-timor-to-get-world-cup-qualification-back-on-track-1.59175|title=Coach Mahdi Ali cautions his players that tougher challenges await as UAE thrash East Timor to get World Cup qualification back on track|author=Amith Passela|publisher=[[The National (Abu Dhabi)|The National]]|date=12 November 2015|access-date=16 November 2015|archive-date=16 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151116013451/http://www.thenational.ae/sport/football/coach-mahdi-ali-cautions-his-players-that-tougher-challenges-await-as-uae-thrash-east-timor-to-get-world-cup-qualification-back-on-track|url-status=live}}</ref>

The next match, Timor-Leste did even worse, suffering a record 0–10 home defeat to a strong [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]] side, who eventually qualified for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup]] in Russia.

Jesse Pinto, an Australian-Timorese footballer, told reporters that the FFTL gives Brazilian players Timorese passports so that they can be registered as "Asian" players and meet the quotas of teams. Pinto also added that the FFTL often takes advantage of players from poor backgrounds, but that it often did not meet its promises of allowing players to travel back to Brazil to meet their families.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-11-08/darwin-based-soccer-player-breaks-silence-on-fifa-allegations/6901784|title=Darwin soccer player who captained Timor-Leste opens up on team's FIFA controversy|author=Jack Kerr|publisher=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation|ABC]]|date=7 November 2015|access-date=13 September 2016|archive-date=31 July 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160731102700/http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-11-08/darwin-based-soccer-player-breaks-silence-on-fifa-allegations/6901784|url-status=live}}</ref>

In December 2016, the FFTL was charged with using forged and falsified documents, fielding ineligible players and bringing the game into disrepute.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2016/12/09/sports/soccer/east-timor-charges-ineligible-players.html?_r=0|title=East Timor Is Accused of Using Ineligible Players for Its Soccer Team|author=Jack Kerr|work=[[The New York Times]]|date=9 December 2016|access-date=9 December 2016|archive-date=17 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180817194858/https://www.nytimes.com/2016/12/09/sports/soccer/east-timor-charges-ineligible-players.html?_r=0|url-status=live}}</ref>

A decision was made on 20 January 2017 that Timor-Leste is barred from participating in the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup qualification|qualification tournament]] for the [[2023 AFC Asian Cup]] after being found to have fielded a total of twelve ineligible players in [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] matches and among other competitions.<ref>{{Cite web |date=January 20, 2017 |title=Federacao Futebol Timor-Leste expelled from AFC Asian Cup 2023 |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/more/news/federacao_futebol_timor-leste_expelled_from_afc_asian_cup_2023.html |access-date=2025-03-09 |website=the-AFC |language=en}}</ref>

==Team image==

=== Kit manufacturers ===
{| class="wikitable"
!Period
!Manufacturer
|-
|2008–2010
|{{flagicon|JPN}} Tiger
|-
|2012
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[Nike, Inc.|Nike]]
|-
|2013–2014
|{{flagicon|GBR}} [[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]]
|-
|2015–2017
|{{flagicon|SIN}} [[Kubba Sportswear]]
|-
|2018–2024
|{{flagicon|INA}} Narrow
|-
|2024–2026
|{{flagicon|INA}} Oliver Sports
|-
|2026–present
|{{flagicon|BRA}} Volt Sport
|}
The team's kit manufacturer was [[Kubba Sportswear]] from June 2014 to February 2017.<ref>{{Cite news|title=Timor Leste Football Federation sign Kubba sportswear contract|url=https://www.facebook.com/pg/KubbaSportswear1/photos/?tab=album&album_id=1460698683943025}}</ref>

Since 2018, the kits are made by Narrow, an Indonesian apparel. The home kit is red shirts, black shorts, and red socks, with black as a secondary color. The away kit all white.<ref>{{Cite web|title=2017-18 Timor-Leste Away Shirt *BNIB*|url=https://www.classicfootballshirts.co.uk/2017-18-timor-leste-away-shirt-bnib.html|website=Сlassic Football Shirts|language=en|access-date=2020-05-22|archive-date=30 July 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200730222525/https://www.classicfootballshirts.co.uk/2017-18-timor-leste-away-shirt-bnib.html|url-status=live}}</ref> From 2008 to 2010, their away kits were yellow but at [[2010 AFF Suzuki Cup qualification]], the away kits changed back into all whites.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.colours-of-football.com/national_teams_kits/afc_asia/tls/timor_leste_1.html|title=Timor-Leste national team|website=www.colours-of-football.com|access-date=2018-09-04|archive-date=4 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180904225627/https://www.colours-of-football.com/national_teams_kits/afc_asia/tls/timor_leste_1.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Their kits were made by Nike in 2012. Previous kit makers include [[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]], [[Joma]], [[Nike Inc.|Nike]] and Tiger.

=== Home stadium ===
Timor Leste's home ground is the [[National Stadium (Timor-Leste)|National Stadium of Timor-Leste]] in [[Dili]]. The stadium capacity is 5,000. The first official match played in the stadium was a [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match against [[Mongolia national football team|Mongolia]] on 12 March 2015. Timor-Leste played their last match at the National Stadium of Timor-Leste against Saudi Arabia on 17 November 2015 and since then, they have used neutral grounds all over Southeast Asia as their home ground.

{| class="wikitable" width=95% style="text-align:center;font-size:100%;"
!colspan=5 style="background: #DC241F; color: #FFC726;|Timor Leste national football team home stadiums
|-
!Image
!Stadium
!Capacity
!Location
!Last match
|-
|[[File:Timor Leste Nacional Stadion.jpg|172x172px]]
|style="text-align:left"|[[National Stadium (Timor-Leste)|National Stadium of Timor-Leste]]
|style="text-align:left"|5,000
|style="text-align:left"|[[Dili]]
|style="text-align:left"|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|KSA}}<br><small>(17 November 2015; [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]])</small>
|}

!Stadium
!Capacity
!Location
!Last match
|-

|[[Kuala Lumpur Stadium]]
|18,000
|[[Cheras]], [[Kuala Lumpur]], [[Malaysia]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|Brunei}}<br><small>(9 June 2026; [[2026 ASEAN Championship qualification]])</small>
|-
|[[File:The Main Stadium for 2009 World Games panorama.jpg|frameless|184x184px]]
|[[National Stadium (Kaohsiung)|Kaohsiung National Stadium]]
|55,000
|[[Zuoying District|Zuoying]], [[Kaohsiung]], [[Taiwan]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|TPE}}<br><small>(17 October 2023; [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC first round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]])</small>
|-
|[[File:Hang Day.jpg|frameless|189x189px]]
|[[Hàng Đẫy Stadium]]
|22,500
|[[Hanoi]], [[Vietnam]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|SGP}}<br><small>(14 December 2024; [[2024 ASEAN Championship]])</small>
|-
|[[File:Stadion Dipta.jpg|frameless|188x188px]]
|[[Kapten I Wayan Dipta Stadium]]
|18,000
|[[Gianyar Regency]], [[Bali]], [[Indonesia national football team|Indonesia]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|MGL}}<br><small>(5 September 2024; [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – play-off round]])</small>
|-
|
|[[Territory Rugby League Stadium]]
|10,000
| [[Darwin, Northern Territory|Darwin]], [[Northern Territory]], [[Australia]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|Maldives}}<br><small>(10 June 2025; [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round]])</small>
|-
|[[File:Chonburi Stadium.jpg|frameless|191x191px]]
|[[Chonburi Stadium]]
|8,680
|[[Mueang Chonburi District|Mueang Chonburi]], [[Chonburi Province]], [[Thailand]].
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|Brunei}}<br><small>(15 October 2024; [[2024 ASEAN Championship qualification]])</small>
|-
|[[File:Track & Field Sports Complex 01.jpg|frameless|186x186px]]
|[[Track & Field Sports Complex]]
|1,700
|[[Bandar Seri Begawan]], [[Brunei]]
|'''v'''&nbsp;&nbsp;{{fb|BRU}}<br><small>(8 November 2022; [[2022 AFF Championship qualification]])</small>
|}

| goals2     =

| referee    = Songkran Bunmeekiart ([[Football Association of Thailand|Thailand]])
| result     = W
}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following 26 players were called up for the [[2026 ASEAN Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/share/v/1HUR9vcurW/|title=LISTA CONVOCADOS TIMOR-LESTE|date=29 June 2026|publisher=[[Football Federation of Timor-Leste]]|via=Facebook}}</ref>

''Caps and goals updated as of 11 June 2026, after the match against [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Dylan Niski|age={{bda|2000|3|1|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Western City Rangers FC|Western City Rangers]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Alexandre Lima|age=2005 (age 21)|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Santa Cruz F.C. (Timor-Leste)|Santa Cruz]]|clubnat=TLS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Egidio Oliveira|age={{bda|2008|12|5|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Karketu Dili F.C.|Karketu Dili]]|clubnat=TLS}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Anizo Correia]]|age={{bda|2003|5|23|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=AD San Antonio|clubnat=TLS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Liam Farrugia|age={{bda|2003|1|13|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Manningham United Blues FC|Manningham United]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=Ryan Jom|age={{bda|2005|3|3|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Canterbury Bankstown FC|Canterbury Bankstown]]|clubnat=AUS}}

*<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad / standby
{{nat fs end}}

==Head-to-head record==
